root_text.iti96:2.1: “Kāmayogayutto, bhikkhave, bhavayogayutto āgāmī hoti āgantā itthattaṁ.
root_text.iti96:2.2: Kāmayogavisaṁyutto, bhikkhave, bhavayogayutto anāgāmī hoti anāgantā itthattaṁ.
root_text.iti96:2.3: Kāmayogavisaṁyutto, bhikkhave, bhavayogavisaṁyutto arahā hoti, khīṇāsavo”ti.
root_text.iti96:6.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti96:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti96:2.1: “Mendicants, one yoked to both sensual pleasures and rebirth is a returner, who comes back to this place.
translation_text.iti96:2.2: One unyoked from sensual pleasures but still yoked to rebirth is a non-returner, who comes not back to this place.
translation_text.iti96:2.3: One unyoked from both sensual pleasures and rebirth is a perfected one, who has ended defilements.”
translation_text.iti96:3.2: and the desire to be reborn in a future life;
translation_text.iti96:4.1: Those who’ve given up sensual pleasures
translation_text.iti96:4.2: without attaining the end of defilements,
translation_text.iti96:4.3: and are still yoked to being reborn,
translation_text.iti96:5.1: Those who have cut off doubt,
translation_text.iti96:5.2: and ended conceit and future lives,
translation_text.iti96:5.3: they’re the ones in this world <j>who’ve truly crossed over,
translation_text.iti96:5.4: having reached the ending of defilements.”
translation_text.iti96:6.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
